name: feat-ESE-0015-05
title: Aspect ratio mismatch handling
description: Implement the three aspect ratio mismatch modes for cameras: stretch, letterbox, and truncate. When a camera's output aspect ratio differs from its destination RenderQuad, the mode controls how the view fits. Implemented as viewport/scissor adjustments before rendering.
acceptanceCriteria:
- stretch: viewport fills entire destination, distorting if needed
- letterbox: fit preserving aspect ratio, background bars on shorter dimension
- truncate: fill preserving aspect ratio, crop excess on longer dimension
- Default is letterbox when aspectRatioMismatch is not specified
- Unit tested with various source/destination aspect ratio combinations
demoDeliverable: Terminal output from unit tests showing viewport calculations for each mismatch mode.
testingScenarios:
- Wide camera (16:9) on tall quad (1:2): letterbox adds bars top/bottom
- Wide camera (16:9) on tall quad (1:2): truncate crops sides
- Wide camera (16:9) on tall quad (1:2): stretch fills entire quad
- Square camera (1:1) on wide quad (2:1): letterbox adds bars left/right
- No mismatch (same ratio): all modes produce identical viewport
stakeholderUnderstanding: Testing the math of the various render target options, before proceeding to render.
